steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

discordance.evidence: Lactate: 2.4 mmol/L (ref 0.5-2.0); White Blood Cells: 15.5 K/uL (ref 4.0-10.0); Absolute Neutrophil Count: 10.3 K/uL (ref 1.6-6.1); ABD: Soft / ND / TTP lower abdomen more so on the left. Marginal rebound. No guarding. Not frankly peritonitic.

alarm_detect.summary: An elevated lactate (2.4 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (WBC 15.5 K/uL) in a patient on pembrolizumab with only mild left lower quadrant tenderness and no peritoneal signs raises concern for a process more serious than the benign exam suggests, warranting caution before assuming a routine diagnosis.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast is directly capable of identifying the anatomic source of the discordant lab-exam picture (elevated lactate, leukocytosis with only mild LLQ tenderness), and the reasoning explicitly names mesenteric ischemia, colitis, and perforation as targets — the very processes that could explain a more serious underlying condition than the benign exam suggests.
